perturbation.original_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the statement on Schedule 13G with respect to the equity securities of Q Biomed, Inc. is, and any amendment thereto signed by each of the undersigned shall be, filed on behalf of each of the undersigned pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the statement on Schedule 13G with respect to the equity securities of Q Biomed, Inc. is, and any amendment thereto signed by each of the undersigned shall be, filed on behalf of each of the undersigned. All filings will be handled according to standard practices.
perturbation.explanation: The change omits the explicit reference to Rule 13d-1(k) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. While the agreement still mandates filing on Schedule 13G, removing the specific legal citation introduces ambiguity about the required compliance standards. This change affects the paragraph where the agreement ensures adherence to specific legal protocols. The contract references a legal rule to be used when filling the agreement, without referencing it, makes the contract ambiguous to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the statement on Schedule 13G with respect to the equity securities of Q Biomed, Inc. is, and any amendment thereto signed by each of the undersigned shall be, filed on behalf of each of the undersigned. All filings will be handled according to standard practices. The undersigned hereby further agree that this Joint Filing Agreement be included as an exhibit to such statement and any such amendment. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others. This Joint Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit reference to Rule 13d-1(k) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, which creates a contradiction with the original text and the contradicted text by introducing ambiguity. The contradicted text continues to reference standard practices and inclusion of the Joint Filing Agreement, but the explicit legal compliance that was initially included is now missing, making the standard practices less defined. This ambiguity can lead to conflict. The removal of a specific legal rule when the rest of the paragraph continues as if it is referencing one, makes the contradiction. This adheres to the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for ensuring the filing. No party assumes responsibility for the accuracy of the information provided by others.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the explicit responsibility for 'timely filing' and for the 'completeness' of their own information. While it still assigns responsibility for 'ensuring the filing', it removes the clear obligation for timely and complete filings, creating a weaker commitment. The original wording specified both timely and completeness, while the replacement only mentions ensuring the filling.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the statement on Schedule 13G with respect to the equity securities of Q Biomed, Inc. is, and any amendment thereto signed by each of the undersigned shall be, filed on behalf of each of the undersigned pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. The undersigned hereby further agree that this Joint Filing Agreement be included as an exhibit to such statement and any such amendment. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for ensuring the filing. No party assumes responsibility for the accuracy of the information provided by others. This Joint Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The original text assigned responsibility for 'timely filing' and 'completeness and accuracy' of information. The changed text narrows this to simply 'ensuring the filing' and disclaims responsibility for the accuracy of information provided by others. While 'ensuring the filing' might implicitly include timeliness, the explicit responsibility for 'timely filing' and the 'completeness and accuracy' of information has been removed. This creates a contradiction within the document, specifically in the first sentence of the contradicted text, where the original agreement established certain responsibilities related to filings, that are now changed in the document.

perturbation.original_text: This Joint Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Joint Filing Agreement will be executed.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows for execution in any number of counterparts, explicitly stating that these taken together constitute the whole agreement. The removal of this phrase creates ambiguity as to whether separate, possibly differing, executed versions are equally valid. This impacts enforcement because it can be questioned whether only one complete document or multiple partial documents are required. This makes it difficult to enforce if each company files the contract with slight differences.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the statement on Schedule 13G with respect to the equity securities of Q Biomed, Inc. is, and any amendment thereto signed by each of the undersigned shall be, filed on behalf of each of the undersigned pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. The undersigned hereby further agree that this Joint Filing Agreement be included as an exhibit to such statement and any such amendment. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others. This Joint Filing Agreement will be executed.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that counterparts of the agreement, when taken together, constitute the entire agreement. This ensures that even if different parties sign different versions, the composite document is binding. The changed text removes this crucial clarification, creating uncertainty. This omission directly contradicts the requirement in the contradicted text that 'this Joint Filing Agreement be included as an exhibit,' because it's unclear which version, if any, represents the definitive agreement, and whether each filing party has to file the exact same agreement. This creates a clear contradiction, as the original text had language that would enforce that the joint filing agreement is enforced even if the counterparts are slightly different. The change in text removes that assurance, creating a strong contradiction.
